stimulus: With the rollout of more fuel-efficient aircraft engines, total fuel consumption across an airline’s fleet might be expected to drop. Aviation analysts have noticed that gains in per-flight efficiency frequently correlate negatively with overall fuel ______blank though, cheaper operating costs tend to encourage carriers to schedule additional flights, raising total demand for jet fuel.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. “Gains…savings” is one independent clause; “cheaper operating costs…jet fuel” is another. The conjunctive adverb “though,” flanked by its required comma, sits at the head of the second clause; a semicolon after “savings” correctly marks the boundary between the two independent clauses.

Choice A is incorrect because a comma alone before “though,” produces a comma splice between the two independent clauses. Choice B is incorrect because no punctuation between the clauses creates a run-on. Choice D is incorrect because a colon must be preceded by a complete clause that introduces what follows, and “though” here is a transitional adverb, not the start of a list or explanation that a colon would license.
